problem.title: Meeting Rooms
problem.description: Given an array of meeting time intervals where intervals[i] = [starti, endi], determine if a person could attend all meetings (i.e., no two meetings overlap).
assumptions: A person can attend all meetings only if no two intervals overlap.
End time is exclusive: [s, e) does not overlap [e, f).

example.explanation: [0,30] overlaps with [5,10]. Sort by start, check consecutive overlaps.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Sorting [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Sort intervals by start time and check whether any two consecutive meetings overlap. If a meeting starts before the previous one ends, a person cannot attend both, so the function returns false. The key insight is that sorting brings potentially conflicting meetings adjacent to each other, so a single linear scan suffices to detect any overlap.
